finding: SEMrush 20260614 'pages with only one internal link' surfaced a content-cannibalization problem: near-identical localized articles auto-created in tight bursts (Jun 7-13). Three 'best mattress for back pain in Los Angeles' articles, two 'best cooling mattress in LA' articles, and a 2026 duplicate of the established king-vs-california-king guide. They were orphaned partly because they post-date the inventory snapshot AND because they compete with each other.

consolidation.reason: already enriched in #443 (literal [LINK] placeholders replaced with real internal anchors)
curated sleep-blog vs legacy beds-mattresses blog
established 2023 article, covered by the ArticleEnrichment component (size-guide cluster)

mechanism: redirect-shadow via redirects-manual.json -> 301 at the edge (next.config redirects), excluded from sitemap (REDIRECT_SOURCES), and skipped in related-articles rotation. Reversible. Shopify articles left published (URLs 301); recommend deleting the 4 duplicate articles in Shopify once redirects deploy.
followups: The 2 KEPT canonicals (back-pain local-buyers-guide, cooling sleep-blog) post-date the 2026-05-25 inventory snapshot, so they have no sibling inbound links yet. The nightly refresh-inventory workflow (04:00 UTC) will add them to the snapshot -> related-rotation links them and the 4 shadowed dupes auto-drop from sitemap.
ROOT CAUSE NOT ADDRESSED: something is auto-generating these localized duplicates (user is handling the source separately per the 20260614 consolidation decision).
